runtimeContext.meaning: Does not require daemon, session, profile, auth source, datastore, or lake access.
Inspects local, configured, session-backed, or automation readiness.
Creates, inspects, or removes reusable authentication material.
Opens, selects, inspects, reopens, or closes local live datastore sessions.
Requires daemon-backed live session or explicit automation inputs when supported.

studyContext.meaning: The command does not use study context.
Datastore-backed command that does not resolve a study.
Operates in one study, resolved from --study or current session study.
Intentionally operates across an explicit set of studies.

Assets, Datafiles, Datasets.statusAndPurpose: Delete all versions of a supported asset through archive/delete policy; requires primary or delegate study custodianship.
Remove explicit DUO overrides for one asset version so effective readback can fall back to study defaults.
List explicit DUO restrictions for one asset version, or effective restrictions with source values when --effective is set.
Replace the explicit DUO override set for one asset version; empty arrays are rejected in favor of asset duo clear.
Fetch one asset.
List assets in the resolved study.
List versions of one asset.
Delete a datafile version or whole datafile asset with managed payload cleanup; requires primary or delegate study custodianship.
Copy a managed datafile payload to a requested path.
List managed datafile assets and optionally their versions.
Show one datafile asset/version.
Search bounded datafile catalog summaries without reading file payloads.

Assets, Datafiles, Datasets.output: Tabular data stream or export file
Assets, Datafiles, Datasets.statusAndPurpose: Read or export dataset data.
Delete a dataset version or whole dataset asset with mandatory lake table cleanup; requires primary or delegate study custodianship.
Export dataset rows to a file or directory-derived file.
List dataset assets and optionally their versions.
Show dataset metadata and variable count; --with-variables includes variable definitions.
Preview dataset rows.
Search bounded dataset catalog summaries; row filtering stays in dataset data/preview/export paths.
Withdraw one faulty dataset version with audited provenance; requires primary or delegate study custodianship.

Authentication, Daemon, Sessions.output: Text or JSON auth artifact summary
Authentication, Daemon, Sessions.statusAndPurpose: Run interactive OAuth and optionally persist reusable auth material.
Query daemon runtime, socket, state, session, and protocol readiness checks.
Query running daemon build identity and protocol compatibility.
List known local sessions, including the Authenticated TRE user for live authenticated sessions.

Authentication, Daemon, Sessions.importantInputs: Issuer, client, subject, expiry, and token env flags: Stored auth path: --profile, --env-file; session name
Authentication, Daemon, Sessions.statusAndPurpose: Open an interactive OAuth-backed live session. Open a live session from stored OAuth material. Open a live session from an injected token.
Show session metadata and live state.

Datastore, Semantic Catalog.importantInputs: Existing datastore/catalog/lake values, temporary lake credential inputs, --super-user, --super-password-env, --yes, optional --format
Datastore, Semantic Catalog.statusAndPurpose: Verify a legacy datastore and write a ready identity binding with managed lake catalog credentials.
Provision datastore metadata, roles, lake catalog state, managed catalog credentials, and the datastore-local identity binding.
Summarize datastore identity, safe binding status, connection metadata, lake state, and content counts.

Datastore, Semantic Catalog.output: Text table or JSON discovery response
Datastore, Semantic Catalog.statusAndPurpose: Discover AHRI TRE datastores on the current PostgreSQL server using metadata-only inspection.
Check datastore reachability and identity-binding verification.

Datastore, Semantic Catalog.importantInputs: Datastore identity, store owner credential env, --super-user, --super-password-env, --yes, optional --format
Datastore, Semantic Catalog.statusAndPurpose: Rotate the managed DuckLake catalog role password without changing ordinary open profiles.

Datastore, Semantic Catalog.output: Text or JSON schema compatibility status
Datastore, Semantic Catalog.statusAndPurpose: Inspect metadata schema version, migration history, missing tables, and DuckLake catalog migration ownership. Report pending/adoptable/blocked migration steps without applying them. Apply supported datastore metadata schema migrations and report before/after status.
Add or validate a semantic domain.
Physically delete an unused domain or block with dependency summary; no archive/retirement semantics.
Fetch one domain.
List semantic domains.
Inspect tags on domains, studies, variables, entities, relations, assets, datafiles, datasets, and versions.
List normalized tag labels through the stable protocol.
Replace the complete tag set for a supported target or clear all tags.

Datastore, Semantic Catalog.importantInputs: Domain, name, value type, optional ontology/vocabulary fields, --format
Datastore, Semantic Catalog.statusAndPurpose: Add a variable definition.
Physically delete an unused variable definition or block while preserving dataset/provenance meaning.
Fetch one variable.
List semantic variables by domain, or by resolved study when no domain is provided.
Search bounded dictionary variable summaries without reading dataset rows.
Update variable metadata.
Add a vocabulary and items.
Physically delete an unused vocabulary definition or block on variables, items, mappings, and history.
Fetch one vocabulary and its items; JSON protocol output exposes items at .data.vocabulary.items.
List vocabularies by domain, or by resolved study when no domain is provided.

Local Commands.statusAndPurpose: Generate completions for the public CLI.
Check local, session, and Automation readiness.
Print one known JSON Schema document.
List stable JSON control-plane schema IDs and the coverage map.

Local Commands.output: Text version or JSON build metadata
Local Commands.statusAndPurpose: Print CLI version and build metadata.

Runtime.runtimeContext: Creates, inspects, or removes reusable authentication material.
Requires either a daemon-backed live session or explicit Automation inputs when the command supports Automation.
Can inspect local, configured, session-backed, or Automation readiness depending on supplied flags.
Does not require a daemon, session, profile, auth source, datastore, or lake access.
Opens, selects, inspects, reopens, or closes local live datastore sessions.

Study, Governance.statusAndPurpose: Register a study and link it to a domain.
Link the current or explicit study to a domain.
Clear current study from session metadata.
Archive by default into the special Archive study, then delete the source study with mandatory lake cleanup.
Replace the study DUO restriction set.
Set the current study for the active session.
